content: Interventions to address food deserts include: (1) Retail interventions: incentives for supermarkets to locate in underserved areas (tax credits, zoning changes), healthy corner store programs converting existing stores, mobile markets and produce trucks; (2) Alternative distribution: farmers markets with SNAP/EBT acceptance, community-supported agriculture subsidies, food cooperatives, online grocery delivery partnerships; (3) Community-based solutions: community gardens, urban agriculture, food hubs, gleaning programs; (4) Policy approaches: zoning for mixed-use development, transit improvements connecting residents to grocery stores, healthy food financing initiatives (e.g., Pennsylvania Fresh Food Financing Initiative); (5) Individual support: SNAP benefits expansion, WIC program, nutrition education (SNAP-Ed). Evidence for health impact is mixed—supermarket introduction alone shows modest effects on consumption without complementary interventions like pricing strategies and nutrition education.
